blocks.content: When they pulled back the sky was still going — red and silver, one after another — and neither of them said anything for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Nothing. I just — I didn't know it would feel exactly like I thought it would.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It means I was right. About you. About all of it. That's the best possible thing.

He pulled her close and she settled against him naturally, his arms around her from behind, her back against his chest, both of them facing the field as Eli's fireworks kept splitting the dark above them.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I'm the filmmaker. I get to frame it.
She laughed again and leaned her head back against his shoulder. His chin came to rest just above her temple.

Above them the sky cracked open again and again. Around them the field blinked with a thousand small lights. And the two of them stayed exactly where they were — Daisy in his arms, his chin at her shoulder — watching the fireworks and the field full of light.
